executive_summary: The ACIC is a Home Affairs portfolio statutory intelligence and information-sharing agency (security/intelligence/enforcement archetype) whose 2025-26 plan is dominated by the response to the 2024 Independent Review and the rollout of a new intelligence operating model. The agency is candid that it must replace legacy technology, uplift cyber posture, mature data governance, and stand up enterprise-wide data capability — all heavily ICT- and capability-driven demand. It is simultaneously bringing ICT and program/project management work in-house under the APS Strategic Commissioning Framework, which constrains some pure-staff-augmentation BD but leaves room for specialist design, assurance and build work. A history of high-profile ICT/program failure (Biometric Identification Services) and ongoing ANAO scrutiny (including a recent travel-compliance audit) create strong latent demand for independent program assurance, governance and internal-control uplift. The new performance framework (reduced from 11 to 6 measures) and a refreshed risk framework also signal evaluation and assurance opportunities, though clearance constraints and insourcing intent require realistic scoping.

strategic_priorities.title: Implement Independent Review recommendations and new operating model
strategic_priorities.description: The agency's stated major focus is implementing the recommendations of the 2024 Independent Review and standing up a new intelligence operating model to deliver more timely and relevant intelligence, contingent on available funding.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Implementing these recommendations and our new intelligence operating model are a major focus for the agency over the coming year.

strategic_priorities.title: Modernise data, technology and ICT
strategic_priorities.description: Replace legacy technology, implement new solutions to operate at pace and scale, enhance cyber security posture, and mature enterprise-wide data capability including data governance, tooling and data fluency.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: We will enhance our cyber security posture, replace legacy technology and implement new solutions to support operating at pace and scale.

strategic_priorities.title: Evolve national policing information systems and NCIS
strategic_priorities.description: Continue developing the National Criminal Intelligence System — adding datasets, improving intelligence exploitation functionality — and sustain the 17 interlinked national policing information systems serving 71,000+ users.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Capability enhancements planned for the National Criminal Intelligence System in 2025-26 focus on adding datasets, improving criminal intelligence exploitation functionality.

strategic_priorities.title: Strengthen regulation of the National Police Checking Service
strategic_priorities.description: Operate as regulator of accredited bodies under a new access agreement (effective 1 January 2025) with stronger controls, and audit at least 10% of accredited bodies for compliance.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: The new agreement introduces stronger controls governing the management of ACIC accredited bodies.

strategic_priorities.title: Workforce capability uplift and insourcing
strategic_priorities.description: Address critical skills gaps in data analytics, AI and cyber, develop a 5-year strategic people plan, and reduce outsourcing of ICT Digital Solutions and Portfolio/Program/Project Management under the APS Strategic Commissioning Framework.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: We expect our targets for 2025-26 to focus on reduced outsourcing of ICT Digital Solutions and Portfolio Program and Project Management.

strategic_priorities.title: Mature performance and risk frameworks
strategic_priorities.description: Embed the updated risk management framework and mature risk culture, and bed down a streamlined performance framework reduced from 11 to 6 measures aligned to Review recommendations.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: The number of measures has been reduced from 11 in 2024-25 to 6 in 2025-26, driven by ongoing internal changes influenced by the Independent Review.

reform_and_change_drivers: 2024 Independent Review of the ACIC and associated Commonwealth law enforcement arrangements driving operating-model and legislative reform
Pending parliamentary approval of comprehensive legislative reform to functions, powers and legislation
New intelligence operating model implementation, iteratively dependent on funding
APS Strategic Commissioning Framework requiring insourcing of ICT and program/project management
New National Police Checking Service accredited-body access agreement (effective 1 Jan 2025) strengthening the regulator role
Rapidly evolving, technology-enabled serious and organised crime threat (estimated $68bn/year cost to the economy)
Updated risk management framework (ISO 31000:2018, Commonwealth Risk Management Policy) requiring embedding
Streamlined performance framework aligned to Review recommendations
challenges_and_risks: Technology and data enterprise risk: failure to implement, manage or secure data/services/systems/tools and stay ahead of emerging technology
Legacy technology requiring replacement and end-of-life system retirement
Security and integrity risk: protecting tradecraft, sensitive capabilities and against insider compromise
People and culture risk: national talent shortages and competition for data analytics, AI and cyber skills
Finance and resources risk: managing financial requirements, contracts, procurement and preventing fraud — with operating model implementation explicitly funding-dependent
Legal and ethical compliance risk with low risk appetite
Demonstrated history of major ICT/program failure (BIS) and ongoing ANAO scrutiny of governance and compliance

news_and_controversies.summary: A 2024 independent review of the ACIC and associated Commonwealth law-enforcement arrangements called for the agency's remit to be narrowed, with commentary that more reform is still needed.
news_and_controversies.implication: A government-commissioned review signals an imminent operating-model/strategy reset — an entry point for organisational design, target-operating-model and reform-implementation work.
news_and_controversies.source: The Strategist (ASPI), 4 Dec 2024 — https://www.aspistrategist.org.au/review-calls-for-narrowing-australian-criminal-intelligence-commission-focus-but-more-needs-to-be-done/ ; APO, Nov 2024 — https://apo.org.au/node/329004 ; Dept of Home Affairs review page

news_and_controversies.summary: The ACIC's ~$50m+ Biometric Identification Services (BIS) program was scrapped after running late and over budget, with about $26m spent on the dumped NEC system (2018-19; outside the 3-year window but the agency's defining ICT controversy).
news_and_controversies.implication: A high-profile major-ICT-procurement failure means strong residual demand and political sensitivity around program assurance, vendor management, business-case rigour and gateway reviews.
news_and_controversies.source: iTnews, 30 Nov 2018 — https://itnews.com.au/news/acic-spent-26m-on-dumped-biometrics-system-516291 ; Canberra Times, 24 Apr 2018; Computerworld

anao_findings.summary: ANAO performance audit 'Compliance with domestic and international travel requirements in the Australian Criminal Intelligence Commission'; contemporaneous reporting referred to public servants breaching travel rules over 200 times (recent, ~Jan 2026).
anao_findings.implication: Fresh adverse audit findings on internal controls/compliance create near-term demand for internal-control uplift, policy remediation and independent assurance support.
anao_findings.source: ANAO — https://www.anao.gov.au/work/performance-audit/compliance-domestic-and-international-travel-requirements-the-acic ; Canberra Times, 22 Jan 2026 (verify specific to ACIC)

anao_findings.summary: ANAO performance audit on the ACIC's administration of the Biometric Identification Services project (2019; outside window) characterised the administration as 'deficient in almost every significant respect.'
anao_findings.implication: Establishes ANAO's continued scrutiny of ACIC ICT/program governance — relevant for positioning around audit-readiness and major-project assurance.

opportunity_areas.area: Operating model design and Review implementation
opportunity_areas.agency_need: The agency must implement Independent Review recommendations and stand up a new intelligence operating model — its stated major focus for the year.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Target operating model design and a Review-response implementation roadmap, defining functions, governance arrangements and transition sequencing, with governance redesign support.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan explicitly names Review-recommendation implementation and a new operating model as the agency's primary focus; the ASPI/APO coverage confirms a government-commissioned reset is underway, creating clear time-bound demand.

opportunity_areas.area: Independent assurance of the operating-model and ICT reform program
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Major reform plus a documented history of ICT/program failure (BIS) and ongoing ANAO scrutiny create demand for independent delivery assurance.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Gateway-style independent assurance and health checks at key milestones of the operating-model and legacy-replacement programs, plus delivery-confidence reviews.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The BIS failure and ANAO's findings ('deficient in almost every significant respect') show heightened scrutiny of ACIC's major-project governance; independent assurance directly addresses this and the Technology and data enterprise risk.

opportunity_areas.area: Legacy technology replacement and NCIS enhancement
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Replace legacy technology, implement new solutions to operate at pace and scale, and enhance the National Criminal Intelligence System (new datasets, intelligence exploitation functionality).
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Discovery and service design, business/systems/process analysis and solutions implementation for legacy replacement and NCIS enhancement, scoped to clearance constraints.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan names specific ICT modernisation and NCIS capability enhancements for 2025-26; the firm can build and implement, not only advise. Note insourcing of ICT Digital Solutions may favour specialist design/build over augmentation.

opportunity_areas.area: Enterprise data governance and analytics uplift
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Enhance enterprise-wide data capabilities including data governance, tooling and data fluency for more efficient exploitation of agency and national data.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Data governance framework design, data quality standards and an information management strategy, with analytics capability uplift aligned to the ICT and Data strategies.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan repeatedly stresses data governance, data quality and analytics as core to the new operating model and lists 'Ensure quality data management across ACIC data systems' as a strategic objective.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Enhance cyber security posture and improve information protection as part of capability integration and the Security and integrity enterprise risk.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Cyber security assessment and assurance of critical national policing/intelligence systems and data, with an advisory uplift roadmap.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan explicitly commits to 'enhance our cyber security posture' and names protection of sensitive capabilities and information security as an enterprise risk for a national intelligence agency.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Critical skills gaps in data analytics, AI and cyber; development of a 5-year strategic people plan and leadership pipelines amid talent shortages.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Strategic workforce planning and a capability uplift program design to support the 5-year people plan, including critical-skills and leadership pipeline strategies.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan details critical skills gaps, an interim 2024-25 workforce plan and intent to develop a 5-year strategic people plan; however insourcing intent means design/advisory is a better fit than resourcing.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Newly streamlined performance framework (11 to 6 measures) aligned to Review recommendations, requiring maturation and validation.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: PGPA-aligned performance-measure review and methodology validation, plus an evaluation of the new operating model's outcomes once implemented.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan states measures were reduced and methodologies are reviewed annually for s16EA compliance; a materially revised framework benefits from independent validation and later evaluation.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Embed the updated risk management framework, mature risk culture and integrate risk into planning processes.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Risk framework embedding support and risk-maturity assessment, with committee-effectiveness review for the Compliance and Risk and Audit and Risk Committees.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan commits to embedding a new risk framework and maturing risk culture in 2025-26, with named governance committees — a credible governance advisory opportunity, though general rather than program-specific.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Recent ANAO travel-compliance findings and a low risk appetite for legal/compliance/integrity matters indicate internal-control pressure.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Internal-control health check and compliance remediation review addressing the travel-requirements findings and broader Finance and resources risk controls.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The ANAO travel-compliance audit (over 200 breaches reported) and the Finance and resources enterprise risk (contracts, procurement, fraud) point to near-term internal-control uplift demand.

opportunity_areas.area: National Police Checking Service regulator support
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Operate as regulator of accredited bodies under a new access agreement, auditing at least 10% of bodies for compliance.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Compliance audit methodology design and regulatory-assurance support for accredited-body audits aligned to RMG 128 Regulator Performance.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Performance measure 6 commits the agency to auditing 10%+ of accredited bodies under a new (Jan 2025) agreement; methodology and assurance support is a credible, proportionate fit.

recommended_actions.action: Approach the executive owning operating-model/Review implementation (CEO office and the relevant Deputy/Group head) with a tailored target-operating-model and Review-response capability statement.
recommended_actions.rationale: Review implementation is the agency's stated top focus this year and is the largest, most time-bound opportunity; early positioning before scoping decisions is critical.

recommended_actions.action: Prepare a major-program assurance pitch referencing the BIS lessons and recent ANAO scrutiny, targeting the Chief Risk Officer and the Audit and Risk Committee.
recommended_actions.rationale: ACIC's documented ICT-program failure and continued ANAO attention make independent gateway-style assurance an easy, lower-sensitivity sell that de-risks the funding-dependent reform program.

recommended_actions.action: Develop a data governance and analytics uplift proposition aligned to the agency's ICT and Data strategies and engage the data/technology capability owner.
recommended_actions.rationale: Data governance, quality and analytics are named strategic objectives and enablers of the operating model — a clearly funded, repeatedly emphasised priority.

recommended_actions.action: Scope offerings around design, assurance and specialist build rather than staff augmentation, given the APS Strategic Commissioning Framework insourcing of ICT and PPM.
recommended_actions.rationale: The plan explicitly targets reduced outsourcing of ICT Digital Solutions and Portfolio/Program/Project Management; mis-pitching augmentation would fail. Specialist design/build/assurance remains viable.

recommended_actions.action: Monitor for the Government response to the 2024 Independent Review and any legislative reform (pending parliamentary approval) as procurement triggers.
recommended_actions.rationale: Implementation status of the Review and legislation will define the pipeline of reform-delivery and assurance work; watching these closes the parliamentary intelligence gap noted in research.

recommended_actions.action: Prepare an internal-control/compliance remediation note referencing the ANAO travel-requirements audit for the COO/CFO function.
recommended_actions.rationale: Fresh adverse compliance findings create near-term, proportionate remediation demand and a credible reason for a first meeting on corporate-enabling work.

engagement_targets: CEO / accountable authority office (owner of Review implementation and operating model)
Deputy Secretary / Group head responsible for the new intelligence operating model
Chief Risk Officer (risk framework embedding, enterprise risk)
Audit and Risk Committee and Compliance and Risk Committee
Chief Information/Technology Officer or ICT & Data strategy owner (legacy replacement, NCIS, cyber, data governance)
Chief Operating Officer / CFO (Finance and resources risk, internal controls, ANAO travel-audit response)
Head of People/Workforce function (5-year strategic people plan, capability uplift)
National Police Checking Service regulatory function lead
talking_points: Implementing the Independent Review and a new operating model is your stated top priority — we design target operating models and Review-response roadmaps that translate recommendations into a sequenced, funding-staged transition.
Major reform plus the BIS history and recent ANAO findings make independent, gateway-style program assurance a low-regret investment that protects funding-dependent delivery.

Your strategic objectives put data quality and governance at the core of the new operating model — we can design the data governance framework, quality standards and analytics uplift that underpin it.
Legacy replacement and NCIS enhancement are named 2025-26 commitments — we can deliver discovery, service design and build, scoped to your clearance and insourcing constraints.
With ICT and PPM moving in-house under the Strategic Commissioning Framework, our value is specialist design, assurance and build — not augmentation — complementing your internal capability.
The streamlined 6-measure performance framework and refreshed risk framework would benefit from independent validation now, and outcome evaluation once the operating model lands.
caveats: The plan is unusually candid about reform, ICT modernisation and data needs, which supports several high-confidence opportunities. However, three factors temper BD optimism: (1) operating-model implementation is explicitly 'iteratively dependent upon available funding', so timing and scale are uncertain; (2) the APS Strategic Commissioning Framework targets reduced outsourcing of ICT Digital Solutions and Portfolio/Program/Project Management — exactly the firm's delivery zones — so offerings must emphasise specialist design, assurance and build over staff augmentation; and (3) as a national intelligence/enforcement agency, security clearance and access constraints will limit who can do sensitive work. Parliamentary research was incomplete (quota-limited) and the parliamentary_matters list is empty rather than exhausted — the ACIC is overseen by the PJC on Law Enforcement and targeted re-searching is advised. The recent ANAO travel-compliance audit should be verified as ACIC-specific before relying on it in conversation.
